{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the most common cells that engulf bacteria. From the documents, we learn that neutrophils are described as the most numerous cells to arrive at the site of infection and are involved in the acute inflammatory response to bacterial infection, phagocytosing and degrading bacteria. Additionally, document [22] states that monocytes, macrophages, and neutrophils are particularly strong phagocytes, playing a more significant role in the innate immune response through their microbicidal and antigen-presenting capabilities. This information strongly supports the idea that neutrophilic leukocytes are the most common cells that engulf bacteria.", "answer_choice": "A"}
